stories.headline: Trump says Israel and Lebanon will extend ceasefire by 3 weeks
stories.summary: President says US Navy will ‘shoot and kill’ any boat laying mines in Strait of Hormuz

stories.headline: Iran War Has Drained U.S. Supplies of Critical, Costly Weapons
stories.summary: The Pentagon’s rush to rearm its Mideast forces makes it less ready to confront potential adversaries like Russia and China, administration and congressional officials say.

stories.headline: US soldier charged after winning $400,000 betting on removal of Maduro
stories.summary: Gannon Ken Van Dyke allegedly made trades on Polymarket on the basis of classified information, the justice department says.

stories.headline: Trump Says Israel and Lebanon Agree to Extend Cease-Fire by Three Weeks
stories.summary: The president made the announcement after he hosted a meeting at the White House of Israeli and Lebanese diplomats.

stories.headline: Oil rises above $106 per barrel as US, Iran deadlocked in Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: Jump in prices comes as Donald Trump says vessels will need permission of US Navy to transit key waterway.

stories.headline: US soldier involved in Maduro raid charged over alleged bets on capture
stories.summary: Gannon Ken Van Dyke, who allegedly made more than $400,000 on Polymarket, could face up to 60 years in prisonA US soldier who played a role in the January capture of Venezuelan president Nicolás Maduro is now in custody after allegedly cashing in over $400,000 on wagers about the politician’s removal from office, federal authorities announced on Thursday.Prosecutors say beginning in early December the soldier, Gannon Ken Van Dyke, was involved in

stories.headline: Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire extended by three weeks, Trump says
stories.summary: Trump said Israel and Lebanon agreed to extend the Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire, which was set to expire on Monday, by three weeks. Meanwhile, the US and Iran persist in its tense standoff in the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.headline: U.S. soldier accused of pocketing $400,000 through bets on Maduro’s capture
stories.summary: The bet on the seizing of the Venezuelan leader has drawn scrutiny to insider trading within the growing prediction market industry.

stories.headline: Volkswagen's Cisek on China Business
stories.summary: Volkswagen is accelerating its China strategy by deepening partnerships with local technology and electric vehicle makers, including Xpeng, as it faces intensifying competition and a prolonged price war. The company is pushing greater localisation across manufacturing, R&D and product development to shorten vehicle cycles and cut costs. The strategy comes as China’s auto market shifts toward replacement buyers and faster innovation, putting p

stories.headline: Seres on Impact of Middle East War
stories.summary: Seres Group President Jonn Zhang discusses how the company is managing risks to its overseas expansion plans, particularly in the Middle East. He speaks to Bloomberg's Stephen Engle at the Beijing Auto Show.  (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: ECB to Hike in June Before Reversing Course in 2027, Poll Shows
stories.summary: The European Central Bank will respond to the Iran war by raising interest rates once — in June — before reversing the move next year to protect economic growth, analysts say.

stories.headline: Oil Rises on Iran Talks Stalemate; Katayama Talks Yen | The Asia Trade 4/24/2026
stories.summary: "Bloomberg: The Asia Trade" brings you everything you need to know to get ahead as the trading day begins in Asia. Bloomberg TV is live from Tokyo and Sydney with Shery Ahn and Haidi Stroud-Watts, getting insight and analysis from newsmakers and industry leaders on the biggest stories shaping global markets. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Thailand's Siam Cement to freeze Vietnam petrochemical plant over Iran war
Beijing's auto bash and Coupang under scrutiny
Trump tells BBC that King's visit could 'absolutely' help repair relations with UK
stories.summary: In a phone interview with the BBC's North America editor, the president discussed next week's visit and his relationship with the UK PM.

stories.headline: Donald Trump and the remaking of America’s media order
stories.summary: The president once railed against the ‘fake news’ from the outside. Today, the system itself bears his imprint

stories.headline: Banks charged sharply different fees for access to Anthropic investment
stories.summary: Disparate pricing raises prospect of investors earning unequal returns in the same deal on the basis of who their banker is

stories.headline: KKR, Capital Group to Start Public-Private Credit Fund in Asia
stories.summary: KKR & Co. and Capital Group plan to launch a public-private credit fund in Asia this year, in the latest tie-up between private-markets specialists and traditional asset managers seeking to attract more retail money.

stories.headline: BlackRock iShares: Market Volatility Boosts ETF Use
stories.summary: Nicholas Peach, APAC Head of iShares at BlackRock, says investors have been using ETFs to express their sentiment amid rapid market-moving news cycles. He speaks on Bloomberg's ETF IQ Asia. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: SBI Funds Is Said to Start Marketing $1.5 Billion IPO Next Week
stories.summary: SBI Funds Management Ltd. is set to begin formal marketing as early as next week for its planned initial public offering to raise as much as $1.5 billion, according to people familiar with the matter.

stories.headline: Geely Senior VP Yang on Business Outlook
stories.summary:
Victor Yang, Senior VP of Zhejiang Geely, discusses the business impact of geopolitics and automaker's outlook for growth.
He speaks with Bloomberg's Stephen Engle on the sidelines of the annual Beijing Auto Show on "Bloomberg: The Asia Trade". (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: SNB Move Below Zero Bigger Step Than Normal Cut: Schlegel Tells NZZ
stories.summary: Swiss National Bank President Martin Schlegel said cutting borrowing costs below zero is a more significant move than a reduction above that level, according to an interview in Neue Zuercher Zeitung.

stories.headline: Nomura's Wang on Asia Market Outlook
stories.summary:
Julia Wang, North Asia CIO of Nomura International Wealth Management, says she "sees risk of high oil price for longer" and discusses her investment strategies for Asia markets.
She speaks with Shery Ahn and Haidi Stroud-Watts on "Bloomberg: The Asia Trade". (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Anthony Albanese accused of ‘caving to gas companies’ as Labor set to reject new export tax

stories.headline: Australia news live: US approves first major Aukus submarine contract; Harvey Norman facing class action for alleged ‘misleading’ ads
stories.summary: Follow today’s news liveGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastAllegra Spender says 25% gas export tax would help fix ‘faulty’ systemIndependent MP Allegra Spender said a 25% tax on gas exports would help rectify what she sees as “faulty” taxation arrangements that have seen an Australian resource sent overseas with minimal benefit to the country.The gas industry is a very profitable industry and pays income tax. stories.headline: ‘If you buy Kyle, you get Kyle’: Sandilands’ lawyer defends sacked Kiis FM shock jock in Sydney court
stories.summary: Radio presenter arrives by Rolls-Royce for case management hearing into allegations he breached his radio contractFollow our Australia news live blog for latest updatesGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastKyle Sandilands’ conduct may not be “nice” but “if you buy Kyle, you get Kyle”, the federal court has heard in a high-profile legal fight the sacked shock jock describes as “pretty ugly”.Sandilands’ barrister, Scott Roberts

stories.headline: Largest-ever ban on toxic chemicals in EU hit by ‘extremely frustrating’ delays
stories.summary: Green groups say European Commission is ‘chief roadblock’ to its own plans, as report finds poor progress four years onHarmful compounds in children’s nappies and toxic “forever chemicals” in everyday products are among 14 hazardous substance groups hit by lengthy delays to EU pollution controls, according to report findings described by scientists as “extremely frustrating”.The European Commission sought to push broad categories of dangerous sub

stories.headline: Thai main opposition leader at risk over lese-majeste reform bid
Vietnam prepares crypto market debut as players line up
India's Russian oil imports double, likely to remain high in May
Intel stock jumps nearly 20% on rising CPU demand amid supply crunch
Buyout firm EQT sets sights on Japan's Kakaku.com, sending stock up 24%
Inside Xiaomi's Beijing factory: The machine behind a smartphone made every 6 seconds
stories.summary: Euronews had a rare chance to step inside Xiaomi's Beijing factory, which is almost fully automated and reveals the manufacturing muscle behind a brand seeking to challenge Apple and Samsung at the premium end of the global smartphone market.

stories.headline: US still delivering weapons to Ukraine despite Iran war, Zelenskyy says
stories.summary: Meanwhile, three people were killed and 10 others wounded in a Russian drone attack on the central city of Dnipro.

stories.headline: EU formally approves 90bn euro Ukraine loan and new sanctions on Russia
stories.summary: Ukrainian President Zelenskyy welcomes the development, urging that the first tranche be disbursed by May or June.

stories.headline: UK prepared to deploy RAF Typhoons to keep strait of Hormuz open after Iran war
stories.summary: Proposal at heart of offer made during a 30-country two-day meeting jointly organised by FranceMiddle East crisis – live updatesBritain is prepared to deploy a squadron of RAF Typhoons based in Qatar to patrol over the strait of Hormuz as part of a multinational mission to keep open the strategic waterway once the Iran war comes to an end.The UK military also offered to deploy mine-hunting drones and specialist divers to help clear the strait min

stories.headline: Iran says Strait of Hormuz cannot be opened due to ceasefire breaches
stories.summary: Iran's chief negotiator says "violations" by the US and Israel make it impossible to open the strait.

stories.headline: EU formally approves €90bn Ukraine loan and 20th sanctions package against Russia
stories.summary: Ursula von der Leyen hails ‘good news’ after Hungary’s lifting of vetoes allows leaders to sign off on agreementsEU leaders have welcomed the end of diplomatic deadlock over a long-awaited €90bn (£78bn) loan for Ukraine, after the bloc completed the agreement along with a 20th sanctions package against Russia.After weeks of delay, the EU signed off on the loan on Thursday, in time for a summit in Cyprus that began in the evening and will include

stories.headline: Israeli strikes kill eight Palestinians in Gaza, first responders say
stories.summary: One strike killed three children and two adults near a mosque in the northern town of Beit Lahia on Wednesday evening.

stories.headline: Lebanon accuses Israel of targeting journalist killed in air strike
stories.summary: Lebanon's prime minister accused Israel of war crimes after IDF attacks on Red Cross vehicles also stopped rescuers from reaching the site.

stories.headline: Ukraine needs full not symbolic membership in the EU, Zelenskyy says
stories.summary: With a €90 billion loan for Kyiv unlocked and a new package of sanctions against Russia going ahead, attention is turning to Hungary’s long-standing veto on Ukraine’s EU accession.

stories.headline: Trump says he is keeping Strait of Hormuz closed, not Iran
stories.summary: Donald Trump said he is the one keeping the Strait of Hormuz closed, not Iran.

stories.headline: Philippines’ ex-president Rodrigo Duterte to face trial for crimes against humanity
stories.summary: ICC judges say there are substantial grounds to believe Duterte guided anti-drugs crackdown that killed thousandsThe former president of the Philippines, Rodrigo Duterte, will face trial at the international criminal court (ICC) after judges unanimously confirmed charges of crimes against humanity over his “war on drugs”.Pre-trial judges concluded on Thursday that there were substantial grounds to believe Duterte was responsible for the crimes ag

stories.headline: Israeli forces kill Palestinian teenager in occupied West Bank raid
stories.summary: Teenager dies in hospital after Israeli forces shot him with live ammunition in the shoulder, Palestinian media reports.

stories.headline: US boards ship carrying Iran oil as Trump threatens mine-laying vessels
stories.summary: Central Command said it has intercepted 33 vessels since imposing a blockade on ships coming to and from Iran.

stories.headline: Philippine ex-president Duterte to stand trial as ICC confirms crimes against humanity charges
stories.summary: Ex-president Rodrigo Duterte is accused of the extrajudicial killing of thousands during his "war on drugs".

stories.headline: US soldier charged with using Polymarket to bet on Nicolas Maduro abduction
stories.summary: The suspect, 38-year-old Gannon Ken Van Dyke, allegedly made $400,000 from insider knowledge of the Venezuela attack.

stories.headline: Woman killed by bear in Polish forest, son and local government say
stories.summary: In an extremely rare fatal encounter, the 58-year-old was killed as she was walking in a mountainous area, her son told rescuers.

stories.headline: US government watchdog to investigate Epstein files release
stories.summary: The justice department's inspector general will review the withholding and overdue release of secret files.

stories.headline: More than 500 people killed in Tanzania election violence, inquiry finds
stories.summary: Opposition parties accused security forces but the official report does not say who was responsible.

stories.headline: Oil Gains on Iran Stalemate, Global Stocks Dip: Markets Wrap
stories.summary: Oil rose for a fifth day as concerns grew that the US and Iran were making little progress toward resuming talks on de-escalation, keeping the Strait of Hormuz effectively closed.

stories.headline: Trump intends to invite Putin to G-20 summit in Miami, officials say
stories.summary: The Russian leader has not yet committed to attend the annual gathering of world leaders, which is scheduled for December at the president’s Doral golf resort.

stories.headline: DOJ watchdog launches review of agency’s compliance with Epstein files law
stories.summary: The audit by the department’s inspector general follows months of lawmaker complaints over the congressionally mandated release of investigative files.

stories.headline: Milei administration in Argentina blocks journalist access to Casa Rosada
stories.summary: Press advocates have warned of hostile rhetoric towards journalists and increasingly restrictive policies under Milei.

stories.headline: South African court orders Zambia to return former president's body
stories.summary: Zambia authorities and Edgar Lungu's family are in a dispute over where the former leader's remains should be buried.

stories.headline: Taiwan minister makes rare visit to disputed South China Sea island
stories.summary: Kuan Bi-ling observed coast guard drills on Taiping Island, also claimed by China, the Philippines and Vietnam.

stories.headline: High-stakes West Bengal election begins as parties row over voter roll
stories.summary: The polls are taking place against a controversy over a sweeping revision of electoral rolls.

stories.headline: Full Interview: Japan’s Katayama on Constant Close Contact With US on FX
stories.summary: Japanese Finance Minister Satsuki Katayama says that she is in close communication with the US on FX. Katayama spoke exclusively with Shery Ahn at the Bloomberg New Voices 2026 event in Tokyo. (Around 5:40 and 7:30, Katayama references a currency cooperation agreement with the US in 2024. The deal was in 2025.)  (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: St Vincent and Grenadines government pauses constitutional amendment bills after public backlash
stories.summary: Bills are seeking to change section that opposition says makes Godwin Friday, a dual citizen, ineligible to be PMThe St Vincent and the Grenadines government has delayed a controversial effort to amend a section of the country’s constitution that the opposition says renders the prime minister ineligible for his position in parliament.Two bills, among six listed for the parliament session on Tuesday this week, were aimed at clarifying a section of

stories.headline: South Africa president suspends police chief over $21m contract
stories.summary: Police chief Fannie Masemola suspended as President Cyril Ramaphosa faces pressure to tackle corruption ahead of polls.

stories.headline: US and Iran 'locked in war of words' says Lyse Doucet
stories.summary: The US confirmed on Tuesday night that the Vice-President's trip to Pakistan for a second round of peace talks has been cancelled.

stories.headline: Iran says it has seized two ships in Strait of Hormuz after vessels attacked
stories.summary: One of the cargo ships was 'heavily damaged' by Iranian forces, a British maritime agency reported on Wednesday.

stories.headline: Trump buys time for Iran deal after frantic day of diplomacy
stories.summary: The US president's decision marked the second time in as many weeks he has backed off a threat to escalate the war, buying more time.

stories.headline: Trump eyes Iran deal with many of the trade-offs he blasted Obama for accepting
stories.summary: As U.S. envoys pursue peace talks, the president confronts persistent sticking points: sanctions relief, enrichment limits and the risk of empowering Tehran.

stories.headline: EU approves €90bn loan for Ukraine as pipeline is turned on ending deadlock
stories.summary: Ukraine says it has reopened the Druzhba pipeline, after months of stalemate over stalled oil supplies to neighbouring Hungary.

stories.headline: El Salvador holds mass trial for 486 alleged members of notorious MS-13 gang
stories.summary: Human rights groups have warned that the collective prosecutions violate due process and block defendants from accessing legal counselA Salvadoran court on Tuesday began a collective trial of 486 alleged gang members, in one of the biggest mass trials under president Nayib Bukele’s crackdown on gang violence through controversial emergency powers.Prosecutors say the charges against alleged members of the Mara Salvatrucha gang, or MS-13, span more

stories.headline: Heatwaves, floods and wildfires pose rising threat to democracy, report finds
stories.summary: Research shows natural hazards linked to climate crisis disrupted 23 elections in 18 countries in 2024Democracy is under mounting threat from the climate crisis, with new analysis documenting how elections are increasingly shaped not only by political forces but also by floods, wildfires and extreme weather.At least 94 elections and referendums across 52 countries have been disrupted by climate-related impacts over the last two decades, researche

stories.headline: Billionaire backer sues Trump family's crypto firm over alleged extortion
stories.summary: Billionaire investor Justin Sun is suing the family's World Liberty crypto venture after spending $45m on its tokens.

stories.headline: Moldovan oligarch jailed in $1bn 'theft of the century' case
stories.summary: Vlad Plahotniuc, once Moldova's richest man, was found guilty of partaking in a fraud which involved around 12% of the country's GDP.

stories.headline: South Korean fighter jets collided due to pilots taking pictures, report finds
stories.summary: One of the pilots has been fined thousands of dollars by the military to cover repair costs.

stories.headline: America’s descent into state capitalism is exaggerated
stories.summary: Particularly by the state

stories.headline: Iran sees mass redundancies from war with US and Israel
stories.summary: The impact is being felt by manufacturers, retailers and the digital sector, amid warnings it could get worse if the war resumes.

stories.headline: A Kashmir tourist hotspot became a bloodbath. A year on, the pain remains unbearable
stories.summary: A year after militants killed 26 people in Indian-administered Kashmir, families are trying to cope with their loss.

stories.headline: Unprecedented ruling finds Hungary's anti-LGBTQ laws in breach of EU values
stories.summary: The EU's top court finds that the reforms breached EU values on a number of levels and broke the founding values of the EU treaty.

stories.headline: Why police are seeking to arrest billionaire K-pop mogul behind BTS
stories.summary: Bang Si-hyuk, who created the supergroup, denies defrauding investors before his $7.3bn company went public.

stories.headline: Student allegedly jailed in China for six years after taking part in pro-democracy protests in Australia
stories.summary: Exclusive: The Australian government has been urged to take stronger action to protect overseas students from political repressionThe Australian government has been urged to take stronger action to protect Chinese international students from political repression by authorities on their return after a Chinese student was allegedly sentenced to six years’ imprisonment for joining pro-democracy protests in Australia.The student, who the Guardian has

stories.headline: Rumen Radev's party wins Bulgarian election
stories.summary: With 87% of the vote now counted, Progressive Bulgaria has secured a majority in parliament.

stories.headline: Victory Giant Surges in Debut After Biggest Hong Kong Listing This Year
stories.summary: Victory Giant Technology Huizhou Co., a Chinese supplier to Nvidia Corp., surged 50% in its Hong Kong trading debut after raising $2.6 billion in the city’s largest listing in seven months.

stories.headline: India has splurged billions on metro trains. But where are the commuters?
stories.summary: Without better last-mile connectivity and affordable fares, metro use is unlikely to improve quickly, say experts.

stories.headline: Chernobyl's last wedding: The couple who married as a nuclear disaster unfolded
stories.summary: Serhiy and Iryna married 40 years ago, unaware a nuclear reactor had exploded less than 3 miles away.

stories.headline: Ceasefire with Israel brings respite to Lebanon, but obstacles to peace remain
stories.summary: The halt to weeks of fighting is being celebrated in Lebanon, but it will be difficult to ensure it lasts.
